---
title: "Introduction to R package `unifiedml` for conformal prediction"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Introduction to R package `unifiedml` for conformal prediction}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

# Example 1

```{r fig.width=7}
library(unifiedml)
library(glmnet)

# Regression example with automatic splitting
set.seed(123)
X <- matrix(rnorm(200), ncol = 4)
y <- 2*X[,1] - 1.5*X[,2] + rnorm(50)

cmod <- ConformalModel$new(glmnet::glmnet, alpha = 0.1)
cmod$fit(X, y, alpha = 0, lambda = 0.1, cal_size = 0.3, seed = 123)
preds <- cmod$predict(X[1:5, ])
print(preds)
```

# Example 2

```{r, fig.width=7}
library(unifiedml)
library(glmnet)

set.seed(123)

# Generate synthetic data
n <- 200
p <- 4
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), ncol = p, dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("X", 1:p)))
y <- 2 * X[, 1] - 1.5 * X[, 2] + rnorm(n)

# Split data into training (50%), calibration (25%), and test (25%)
n_train <- floor(0.5 * n)
n_cal <- floor(0.25 * n)

indices <- sample(seq_len(n))
train_idx <- indices[1:n_train]
cal_idx <- indices[(n_train + 1):(n_train + n_cal)]
test_idx <- indices[(n_train + n_cal + 1):n]

# Create the splits
X_train <- X[train_idx, ]
y_train <- y[train_idx]

X_cal <- X[cal_idx, ]
y_cal <- y[cal_idx]

X_test <- X[test_idx, ]
y_test <- y[test_idx]

# Create conformal model
cmod <- ConformalModel$new(glmnet::glmnet, alpha = 0.1)

# Fit using training and calibration sets
cmod$fit_calibrate(
  X_train = X_train,
  y_train = y_train,
  X_calib = X_cal,
  y_calib = y_cal,
  alpha = 0,      # glmnet alpha parameter (0 = ridge)
  lambda = 0.1    # regularization parameter
)

# Print model summary
cmod$print()

# Make predictions on test set
test_predictions <- cmod$predict(X_test, type = "interval")
head(test_predictions)

# Evaluate coverage on test set
evaluation <- cmod$evaluate(X_test, y_test)
cat("\n=== Coverage Evaluation ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Target coverage: %.1f%%\n", 100 * (1 - cmod$alpha)))
cat(sprintf("Empirical coverage: %.1f%%\n", 100 * evaluation$coverage))
cat(sprintf("Mean interval width: %.3f\n", evaluation$mean_width))

# Visualize calibration scores
cmod$plot_scores()

# Visualize prediction intervals on test data
cmod$plot_intervals(X_test, y_test)
```

# Example 3

```{r, fig.width=7}
library(randomForest)

# Load and prepare data
data(iris)
# Use binary classification for simplicity
iris_bin <- iris[iris$Species %in% c("setosa", "versicolor"), ]
Xc <- as.matrix(iris_bin[, 1:4])
yc <- as.factor(as.character(iris_bin$Species))

set.seed(123)
n <- nrow(Xc)

# Split data into training (50%), calibration (25%), and test (25%)
n_train <- floor(0.5 * n)
n_cal <- floor(0.25 * n)

indices <- sample(seq_len(n))
train_idx <- indices[1:n_train]
cal_idx <- indices[(n_train + 1):(n_train + n_cal)]
test_idx <- indices[(n_train + n_cal + 1):n]

# Create the splits
X_train_cls <- Xc[train_idx, ]
y_train_cls <- yc[train_idx]

X_cal_cls <- Xc[cal_idx, ]
y_cal_cls <- yc[cal_idx]

X_test_cls <- Xc[test_idx, ]
y_test_cls <- yc[test_idx]

# Create conformal model with randomForest
cmod2 <- ConformalModel$new(randomForest::randomForest, alpha = 0.1)

# Fit using training and calibration sets
cmod2$fit_calibrate(
  X_train = X_train_cls,
  y_train = y_train_cls,
  X_calib = X_cal_cls,
  y_calib = y_cal_cls,
  ntree = 100,
  importance = TRUE
)

# Print model summary
cmod2$print()

# Get prediction sets on test data
prediction_sets <- cmod2$predict(X_test_cls, type = "set")

# Show first 10 prediction sets
cat("\nFirst 10 prediction sets:\n")
for (i in 1:min(10, length(prediction_sets))) {
  cat(sprintf("Sample %2d: True = %-10s | Set = {%s}\n",
              i, as.character(y_test_cls[i]),
              paste(prediction_sets[[i]], collapse = ", ")))
}

# Get point predictions
point_predictions <- cmod2$predict(X_test_cls, type = "point")
cat("\nConfusion Matrix:\n")
print(table(Actual = y_test_cls, Predicted = point_predictions))

# Get probabilities
probabilities <- cmod2$predict(X_test_cls, type = "prob")
head(probabilities, 10)

# Evaluate coverage on test set
evaluation_cls <- cmod2$evaluate(X_test_cls, y_test_cls)
cat("\n=== Coverage Evaluation ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Target coverage: %.1f%%\n", 100 * (1 - cmod2$alpha)))
cat(sprintf("Empirical coverage: %.1f%%\n", 100 * evaluation_cls$coverage))
cat(sprintf("Mean set size: %.2f\n", evaluation_cls$mean_set_size))
cat("\nSet size distribution:\n")
print(table(evaluation_cls$set_sizes))

# Visualize score distribution
cmod2$plot_scores()
```

# Example 4

```{r}
# Function to evaluate a model with conformal prediction
evaluate_conformal_model <- function(model_fn, X_train, y_train, X_cal, y_cal, X_test, y_test, ...) {
  cmod <- ConformalModel$new(model_fn, alpha = 0.1)
  cmod$fit_calibrate(X_train, y_train, X_cal, y_cal, ...)
  eval <- cmod$evaluate(X_test, y_test)
  return(list(
    coverage = eval$coverage,
    mean_width = if (!is.null(eval$mean_width)) eval$mean_width else eval$mean_set_size,
    model = cmod
  ))
}

# Compare ridge regression and lasso
ridge_result <- evaluate_conformal_model(
  glmnet::glmnet, X_train, y_train, X_cal, y_cal, X_test, y_test,
  alpha = 0, lambda = 0.1
)

lasso_result <- evaluate_conformal_model(
  glmnet::glmnet, X_train, y_train, X_cal, y_cal, X_test, y_test,
  alpha = 1, lambda = 0.05
)

cat("=== Model Comparison ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Ridge  - Coverage: %.3f, Avg interval width: %.3f\n",
            ridge_result$coverage, ridge_result$mean_width))
cat(sprintf("Lasso  - Coverage: %.3f, Avg interval width: %.3f\n",
            lasso_result$coverage, lasso_result$mean_width))
```
